title: rottlera tokbrai
constituents: Rottlera tokbrai has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which possess significant medicinal properties. These constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of various diseases and improve overall health. The flavonoids present in Rottlera tokbrai may also contribute to the reduction of cardiovascular risk factors, such as hypertension and atherosclerosis, while its alkaloids have been reported to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle cramps. By harnessing the medicinal constituents of Rottlera tokbrai, researchers aim to develop new treatments for a range of conditions, ultimately improving the quality of life for individuals around the world.
side_effects: Rottlera tokbrai can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can lead to liver damage, seizures, and even coma. The plant's toxic properties can also cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, especially when ingested in large quantities. Furthermore, excessive exposure to the plant's sap can lead to respiratory problems, such as asthma-like symptoms and bronchitis.
